title: calydorea undulata
intro: Calydorea undulata, also known as the Mexican Shellflower or Calydorea, is a perennial flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its vibrant orange and yellow flowers. Calydorea undulata is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids and glycosides, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Calydorea undulata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some folk remedies to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the source and preparation method.
